original_abstract: Small object detection in remote sensing imagery remains a significant challenge due to dense background noise, limited pixel information, and the diminutive scale of targets. While modern deep learning frameworks have advanced general object detection, their performance on these specialized datasets often falls short. In this paper, we propose LAR-YOLOv8, an enhanced detection framework designed specifically to address these limitations. Our methodology introduces three primary innovations: first, we integrate a dual-branch attention mechanism and Vision Transformer blocks into the backbone to refine local feature extraction and maximize global feature representation. Second, we develop an attention-guided bi-directional feature pyramid network (ABFPN) that utilizes a dynamic sparse attention mechanism to capture high-resolution details from shallow layers while employing top-down paths to supervise multi-scale feature fusion. Finally, we introduce the RIOU loss function, which improves bounding box regression by ensuring better shape consistency and preventing loss degradation in complex scenarios. Extensive evaluations on the NWPU VHR-10, RSOD, and CARPK datasets demonstrate that LAR-YOLOv8 achieves superior mean Average Precision (mAP), particularly for small objects, while maintaining a competitive balance between parameter count and real-time processing speeds. These results validate the efficacy of the proposed architectural enhancements for robust remote sensing analysis.
humanized_abstract: Small object detection from remote sensing images is still a difficult task, which is often accompanied with intense background clutter, insufficient spatial information of the pixels, and small scale of the target objects. Generally, state-of-the-art object detection models based on deep learning are not effective for such small object detection task. In this work, we propose a novel detection framework named LAR-YOLOv8 by making full use of images spatial information. The main improvements are three folds. First, we modify the YOLOv8 backbone by replacing the neck structure with a mix of a dual-branch attention mechanism and Vision Transformer (ViT) blocks, aiming to enhance the local information and global feature. Second, we apply an attention-guided bi-directional feature pyramid network (ABFPN), with a dynamic sparse attention mechanism to extract the fine-granularity information in the shallow layers, while utilizes top-down paths to guide the multi-scale feature fusion process. Third, we utilize the RIOU loss to conduct a regression of the bounding boxes, aiming to keep the shape of the bounding box precise and avoid loss being lost in complex cases. Experimental results on the benchmark datasets of NWPU VHR-10, RSOD and CARPK show that our proposed LAR-YOLOv8 performs best mean Average Precision (mAP) on small object detection, meanwhile keeps a moderate number of parameters and maintains real-time speed.
